title: Athyrium drepanophyllum
intro: Athyrium drepanophyllum, also known as Japanese Lady Fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia, characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds and unique leaf structure. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve immune function, and protect against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Athyrium drepanophyllum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Athyrium drepanophyllum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Athyrium drepanophyllum is classified under the family Athyriaceae. This species is a type of fern, specifically a member of the genus Athyrium. It belongs to the division Pteridophyta and the phylum Pteridophyta. The kingdom to which Athyrium drepanophyllum belongs is Plantae. Within the plant kingdom, its classification is further divided into the subkingdom Viridiplantae.
